name: Dr Temuulen "Teki" Sankey
school: School of Informatics, Computing, Cyber Systems
focus: Remote sensing • Ecosystem change • UAV and LiDAR mapping
bio: Dr. Temuulen Sankey is a Professor in the School of Informatics, Computing and Cyber Systems as well as the School of Earth and Sustainability at Northern Arizona University, where she has been a faculty member since 2014. Her research focuses on spaceborne and airborne remote sensing to better understand environmental change in arid and semi-arid ecosystems. Dr. Sankey’s work integrates lidar, optical, and thermal infrared remote sensing with spatial statistics and predictive modeling, including machine learning applications in environmental science. She is also a leader in the use of UAV (drone) technology, developing monitoring protocols and high-resolution mapping techniques to assess vegetation structure and composition as well as land surface dynamics. Her research addresses key issues in dryland ecosystems, including vegetation dynamics, climate change impacts, restoration of disturbed landscapes, and fire effects in grassland and shrubland systems. Dr. Sankey earned her Ph.D. and M.S. in Land Resources and Environmental Science from Montana State University and her B.S. from the Mongolian University of the Humanities.
